plan.description: This DMP describes the data management procedures implement in the "Playback the music of the brain - decoding emotions elicited by music in the human brain" project.
plan.title: Playback the music of the brain - decoding emotions elicited by music in the human brain

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

referenceField.label: Funding organizations
referenceField.description: Select a funder of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Grants
referenceField.description: Find the grant of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Project
referenceField.description: Projects in Argos are perceived as distinct activities falling under a grant or common activities under different grants in collaborative schemas, eg open call for contributions. Please complete it for the grant associated to your organization if your project falls under this category. In all other cases, please leave blank and it will be autocompleted.

systemField.label: Access Rights
systemField.description: Choose how the DMP is displayed after is published on Zenodo. By choosing Open Access, the DMP will be open on Zenodo after the Publication Date. By choosing Restricted Access, the DMP will be restricted after the publication is made.

referenceField.label: License
referenceField.description: Assign a license to your DMP by selecting the most appropriate from the list.

reference.label: Centro de Imagem Biomédica e Investigação Translacional
Concurso para Projetos de I&D em Todos os Domínios Científicos - 2021
Playback the music of the brain - decoding emotions elicited by musical sounds in the human brain (fct_________::EXPL/PSI-GER/0948/2021)
Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia, I.P.||FCT

description.description: Categorization of music excerpts based on the affection model (valence and arousal)
description.label: Questionnaire on a music listening task

descriptionTemplate.label: FCT - Template in English
descriptionTemplate.description: DMP template of FCT – English version

fieldSet.title: Will you re-use existing data for this project?
Which existing data you will re-use and under which terms of use?
fieldSet.description: Explain which existing data you will re-use and state any constraints on re-use of existing data if there are any.

fieldSet.title: Describe the data type you will re-use
fieldSet.description:
Give details on the kind of data, for example:
- numeric (databases, spreadsheets);
- textual (documents);
- image;
- audio;
- video;
- and/or mixed media.

labelData.label: If relevant please justify the format selection

fieldSet.title: Describe the format of data you will re-use
fieldSet.description:
Give details on the data format: the way in which the data is encoded for storage, often reflected by the filename extension (for example pdf, xls, doc, txt, or rdf).
Justify the use of certain formats. For example, decisions may be based on staff expertise within the host organisation, a preference for open formats, standards accepted by data repositories, widespread usage within the research community, or on the software or equipment that will be used.
Give preference to open and standard formats as they facilitate sharing and long-term re-use of data. Several repositories provide lists of such ‘preferred formats'.

section.title: What existing data will be re-used?

fieldSet.title: Will new data be created in this project?
Describe the data you expect your research will generate.
fieldSet.description:
Give details on the kind of data, for example:
- numeric (databases, spreadsheets);
- textual (documents);
- image;
- audio;
- video;
- and/or mixed media.

labelData.label: If relevant please justify the format selection

fieldSet.title: Describe the data format you expect your research will generate.
fieldSet.description:
Give details on the data format: the way in which the data is encoded for storage, often reflected by the filename extension (for example pdf, xls, doc, txt, or rdf). Justify the use of certain formats. For example, decisions may be based on staff expertise within the host organisation, a preference for open formats, standards accepted by data repositories, widespread usage within the research community, or on the software or equipment that will be used.

Give preference to open and standard formats as they facilitate sharing and long-term re-use of data. Several repositories provide lists of such ‘preferred formats'.

section.title: What data will be created or produced?

labelData.label: If relevant please justify the volume estimate

fieldSet.title: How much data storage will your project require in total?

section.title: What is the data volume?

fieldSet.title: Indicate what documentation will accompany the data.
fieldSet.description:
Consider what other documentation is needed to enable re-use. This may include information on the methodology used to collect the data, analytical and procedural information, definitions of variables, units of measurement, and so on. 

Consider how this information will be captured and where it will be recorded, for example in a database with links to each item, a ‘readme’ text file, file headers, code books, or lab notebooks. 

Indicate how the data will be organised during the project, mentioning for example conventions, version control, and folder structures. Consistent, well-ordered research data will be easier to find, understand, and re-use.

section.title: Documentation

fieldSet.title: Is there metadata associated with the data?
Indicate which metadata will be provided to help others identify and discover the data.
fieldSet.description: To be findable, accessible, interoperable and reusable, data must be accompanied with descriptive information in the form of metadata.

referenceTypeData.label: Please select the applicable options for used metadata

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Please identify the metadata standard(s) that apply.
fieldSet.description:
Where these are in place, researchers are advised to use community metadata standards. The Research Data Alliance maintains a Directory of Metadata Standards (http://rd-alliance.github.io/metadata-directory/).

Depositing data in a certified or trustworthy repository will typically involve providing information about the data according to a metadata standard scheme (typically Dublin Core or DataCite Metadata Schema). If this is the case for the data described in this plan, that can be specified here. 

Contact your university library and/or other institutional RDM support staff for further advice on metadata.

referenceTypeData.label: Please select the applicable options for used vocabularies

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Please identify the controlled vocabularies that apply.

labelData.label: Please fill in the appropriate language for metadata according to ISO 639-3 (e.g. eng, por).

fieldSet.title: Please select the appropriate language for metadata.
fieldSet.description: Please add the language of the selected metadata standard according to ISO 639-3.

section.title: Metadata

page.title: STORAGE AND SECURITY OF DATA AND METADATA

fieldSet.title: Describe where the data and metadata will be stored during the project.
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Give preference to the use of robust, managed storage with automatic backup, such as provided by IT support services of your home institution. Storing data on laptops, stand-alone hard drives, or external storage devices such as USB sticks is not recommended. 

Some research institutions have networked research drives, which offer ample storage space and data security for most purposes. Please specify if you make use of other storage solutions for storage and backup of research data during the project, in addition to or instead of the institutional research drive.

fieldSet.title: Is there a policy in place for data backups during the project?
fieldSet.description: Please specify if there is any policy to use other storage solutions for storage and backup of data during the project, other than, or instead of, your institution's network.

selectData.label: Select the backup type from the list

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Please describe the type of backup.

selectData.label: Select the backup frequency from the list.

fieldSet.title: Please describe the frequency of backup.

section.title: How will data and metadata be stored and backed up during the research?

visibilityRule.value: Serão aplicadas as medidas de segurança que estejam implementadas por defeito na rede da Instituição
Serão aplicadas medidas de segurança adicionais (por favor especificar)

options.label: Default security measures of the institution networked research storage
options.value: Default security measures of the institution networked research storage

fieldSet.title: How will data security and protection of sensitive data be taken care of during the research?
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Consider data protection, particularly if your data is sensitive – for example, containing personal data, politically sensitive information or information relating to religion and health, trade secrets or national security information. Describe the main risks and how these will be managed.

Explain how the data will be recovered in the event of an incident.

Explain who will have access to the data during the research and how access to data is controlled, especially in collaborative Partnerships.

Explain which institutional data protection policies are in place.

section.title: Protection and security of sensitive data

page.title: PERSONAL DATA, INTELECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND OWNERSHIP

fieldSet.title: Will you process and/or store personal data during your project?
How will compliance with legislation and (institutional) regulation on personal data be ensured?
fieldSet.description:
FCT strongly recommends that you seek advice from specialised support staff (DPO or equivalent) at your institution.

Ensure that when dealing with personal data data protection laws (for example GDPR) are complied with:
   -  Gain informed consent for preservation and/or sharing of personal data.
   -  Consider anonymisation of personal data for preservation and/or sharing (truly   anonymous data are no longer considered personal data).
   - Consider pseudonymisation of personal data (the main difference with anonymisation is that pseudonymisation is reversible).
    - Consider encryption which is seen as a special case of pseudonymisation (the encryption key must be stored separately from the data, for instance by a trusted third party).
    - Explain whether there is a managed access procedure in place for authorised users of personal data.

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: How will ownership of the data and intellectual property rights to the data be managed?
fieldSet.description:
Explain who will be the owner of the data, meaning who will have the rights to control access. 

Make sure to cover these matters of rights to control access to data for multi-partner projects and multiple data owners in the consortium agreement.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Responsible(s)”

section.title: How will you handle issues regarding the processing of personal information and intellectual property rights and ownership?

options.label: All data resulting from the project will be preserved for at least 10 years
options.value: All data resulting from the project will be preserved for at least 10 years

fieldSet.title: How will data be selected for long-term preservation?
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Indicate what data must be retained or destroyed for contractual, legal, or regulatory purposes.
Indicate how it will be decided what data to keep. 
Describe the data to be preserved long-term.

section.title: Long-term data preservation

options.label: All data resulting from the project will be made available
options.value: All data resulting from the project will be made available

fieldSet.title: What data will be made available for re-use?
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Indicate what data will be made available for re-use. 
This selection may differ from the data that is preserved, when the data are so large that it is unfeasible to deposit the data in a repository in its entirety, or if there are reasons that prohibit making data available for re-use. 
If there are any restrictions on the re-use of the data, or if it is necessary to restrict access to certain parts of the data or to apply a data sharing agreement, explain how and why. 
Explain what actions will be taken to overcome or to minimize restrictions
As much as possible, research data should be made publicly available for re-use. 

As a minimum, FCT requires that the data underpinning research papers should be made available to other researchers at the time of the article’s publication, unless there are valid reasons not to do so. The guiding principle here is 'as open as possible, as closed as necessary.' Due consideration is given to aspects such as privacy, public security, ethical limitations, property rights and commercial interests.

options.label: Data available as soon as archived in repository
options.value: Data available as soon as archived in repository

options.label: Data available as soon as article is published
options.value: Data available as soon as article is published

options.label: Data available upon completion of the project
options.value: Data available upon completion of the project

options.label: Data available after completion of project (with embargo)
options.value: Data available after completion of project (with embargo)

fieldSet.title: When will the data be available for re-use, and for how long will the data be available?
fieldSet.description:
Explain when the data will be made available. Indicate the expected timely release. 
Explain the reason and duration of any embargo periods. 
Explain whether exclusive use of the data will be claimed and if so, why and for how long. Indicate whether data sharing will be postponed or restricted for example to publish, protect intellectual property, or seek patents. 

As a minimum, FCT requires that the data underpinning research papers should be made available to other researchers at the time of the article’s publication, unless there are valid reasons not to do so.

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: In which repository will the data be archived and made available for re-use, and under which license?
fieldSet.description:
Indicate where the data will be deposited and made available for re-use. 
Repository Finder can help you find an appropriate repository to deposit your research data. 

Typically, appropriate data repositories adhere to the following minimum selection criteria:
  • provision of persistent and unique identifiers; 
  • use of metadata standards that are broadly accepted by the scientific community; 
  • provision of information that is publicly available; 
  • enabling access to data under well-specified conditions and following open and standard access protocols;
  • provision of information about licenses and permissions; ensuring persistence of data and metadata.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Repositories”

options.label: The repository is registered in the re3data directory
options.value: The repository is registered in the re3data directory

options.label: The repository is aggregated in OpenAIRE
options.value: The repository is aggregated in OpenAIRE

fieldSet.title: Please confirm if:

selectData.label: Does the repository support persistent identifiers?
Please identify the persistent identifier system from the list

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Please indicate whether a persistent identifier will be pursued.
fieldSet.description: Indicate whether a persistent identifier will be pursued. Typically, a trustworthy, long-term repository will provide a persistent identifier.

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually
Please state the date in which the license goes into effect

fieldSet.title: Under which license will the data be archived and made available for re-use?
fieldSet.description: Indicate under which license the data may be re-used. Check the commonly used Creative Commons licenses.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Licences”

fieldSet.title: Describe your strategy for publishing the analysis software that will be generated in this project.
fieldSet.description:
Indicate whether potential users need specific tools or software (technical resources, such as specific scripts, codes or algorithms developed during the project) to access, interpret and (re-)use the data.

Indicate how these items will be made available. 

Consider the sustainability of software needed for accessing and interpreting the data. Check the Five Recommendations for FAIR Software.

section.title: Data available for re-use

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually
Please specify the roles and responsibilities

fieldSet.title: Who (for example role, position, and institution) will be responsible for data management?
fieldSet.description:
Outline the roles and responsibilities for data management/stewardship activities, for example:
  • data capture;
  • metadata production;
  • data quality;
  • storage and backup;
  • data archiving;
  • and data sharing. 
For collaborative projects, explain the coordination of data management responsibilities across partners. 

Indicate who is responsible for implementing the DMP, and for ensuring it is reviewed and, if necessary, revised. 

Consider regular updates of the DMP.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Responsible(s)”

fieldSet.title: What resources (for example financial and time) will be dedicated to data management and ensuring that data will be FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable)?
fieldSet.description:
Explain how the necessary resources (for example time) to prepare the data for sharing/preservation (data curation) have been costed in. 

Consider and justify any resources needed to deliver the data. These may include storage costs, hardware, staff time, costs of preparing data for deposit, and repository charges. 

Indicate whether additional resources will be needed to prepare data for deposit or to cover any charges from data repositories. If yes, explain how much is needed and how such costs will be covered. 

Please elaborate on the budget in your FCT grant application, if appropriate.

section.title: Responsibilities and resources for data management

reference.label: Universidade de Coimbra Instituto de Ciências Nucleares Aplicadas à Saúde

field.textValue: Data will be stored at CIBIT, a research unit of the University of Coimbra. Data managing duties are responsibility of the PI, Bruno Direito. Data is owned by the University of Coimbra.
music files format
wav

textListValues.textListValue: All data resulting from the project will be preserved for at least 10 years

field.textValue: We will add a README.md file explaining the variables and data acquisition protocol.

The institution has a centralized VNA infrastructure (commercial solution by Siemens) that implements redundancy. There is no policy on data backup (either on-site and/or off-site). The raw data is kept in its original form in the VNA, and data is copied for statistical analysis.
Bruno Direito is responsible for data acquisition and data quality assurance and Ana Rodrigues is responsible for checking compliance with regulatory aspects.
CC0
audio

textListValues.textListValue: Data available as soon as article is published

field.textValue: We will publish the data without resctrictions as soon as the first publication is published in a open peer-reviewed journal.
mixed media (textual)
Considering the volume of data and current practices of the institution, most data managing tasks have been included in internal procedures and guidelines.

This project is based on the characterization of music excerpts and their neural correlates based on functional magnetic resonance imaging.
We will save behavioral information (participants categorization of the stimuli) in csv files.
.csv files

textListValues.textListValue: All data resulting from the project will be made available

field.textValue: We aim to acquire data from approximately 50 participants - each participant requires 1MB
All processing scripts will be published in our intitutional GitHub account - https://github.com/CIBIT-UC.
OpenNeuro

All participants signed an informed consent before data acquisition (previously approved by an ethics committee). Raw data is stored in a certified, commercial solution (Vendor Neutral Archive) with access policies in-place - Only approved personnel has access to project's data. All secondary copies (data extracted) is pseudo-anonymized. Only the Principal Investigator (PI) has access to the conversion table (id - project code). The table will be destroyed after the project end.

textListValues.textListValue: The repository is registered in the re3data directory
The repository is aggregated in OpenAIRE

field.textValue:
We will use the music excerpts dataset proposed by Panda et al. (2020).
Panda, Renato, Ricardo Malheiro, and Rui Pedro Paiva. “Novel Audio Features for Music Emotion Recognition.” IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing 11, no. 4 (October 1, 2020): 614–26. https://doi.org/10.1109/TAFFC.2018.2820691.

description.description: Raw MRI data, physiological data and metada using BIDS standard acquired during a music listening paradigm.
description.label: Neural correlates of music listening

descriptionTemplate.label: FCT - Template in English
descriptionTemplate.description: DMP template of FCT – English version

- and/or mixed media.

fieldSet.title: Describe the format of data you will re-use
fieldSet.description:
Give details on the data format: the way in which the data is encoded for storage, often reflected by the filename extension (for example pdf, xls, doc, txt, or rdf).
Justify the use of certain formats. For example, decisions may be based on staff expertise within the host organisation, a preference for open formats, standards accepted by data repositories, widespread usage within the research community, or on the software or equipment that will be used.
Give preference to open and standard formats as they facilitate sharing and long-term re-use of data. Several repositories provide lists of such ‘preferred formats'.

Give details on the data format: the way in which the data is encoded for storage, often reflected by the filename extension (for example pdf, xls, doc, txt, or rdf). Justify the use of certain formats. For example, decisions may be based on staff expertise within the host organisation, a preference for open formats, standards accepted by data repositories, widespread usage within the research community, or on the software or equipment that will be used.

Give preference to open and standard formats as they facilitate sharing and long-term re-use of data. Several repositories provide lists of such ‘preferred formats'.

fieldSet.description:
Consider what other documentation is needed to enable re-use. This may include information on the methodology used to collect the data, analytical and procedural information, definitions of variables, units of measurement, and so on. 

Consider how this information will be captured and where it will be recorded, for example in a database with links to each item, a ‘readme’ text file, file headers, code books, or lab notebooks. 

Indicate how the data will be organised during the project, mentioning for example conventions, version control, and folder structures. Consistent, well-ordered research data will be easier to find, understand, and re-use.

fieldSet.title: Is there metadata associated with the data?
Indicate which metadata will be provided to help others identify and discover the data.
fieldSet.description: To be findable, accessible, interoperable and reusable, data must be accompanied with descriptive information in the form of metadata.

referenceTypeData.label: Please select the applicable options for used metadata

fieldSet.title: Please identify the metadata standard(s) that apply.
fieldSet.description:
Where these are in place, researchers are advised to use community metadata standards. The Research Data Alliance maintains a Directory of Metadata Standards (http://rd-alliance.github.io/metadata-directory/).

Depositing data in a certified or trustworthy repository will typically involve providing information about the data according to a metadata standard scheme (typically Dublin Core or DataCite Metadata Schema). If this is the case for the data described in this plan, that can be specified here. 

Contact your university library and/or other institutional RDM support staff for further advice on metadata.

section.title: Metadata

page.title: STORAGE AND SECURITY OF DATA AND METADATA

fieldSet.title: Describe where the data and metadata will be stored during the project.
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Give preference to the use of robust, managed storage with automatic backup, such as provided by IT support services of your home institution. Storing data on laptops, stand-alone hard drives, or external storage devices such as USB sticks is not recommended. 

Some research institutions have networked research drives, which offer ample storage space and data security for most purposes. Please specify if you make use of other storage solutions for storage and backup of research data during the project, in addition to or instead of the institutional research drive.

fieldSet.title: Is there a policy in place for data backups during the project?
fieldSet.description: Please specify if there is any policy to use other storage solutions for storage and backup of data during the project, other than, or instead of, your institution's network.

fieldSet.title: How will data security and protection of sensitive data be taken care of during the research?
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Consider data protection, particularly if your data is sensitive – for example, containing personal data, politically sensitive information or information relating to religion and health, trade secrets or national security information. Describe the main risks and how these will be managed.

Explain how the data will be recovered in the event of an incident.

Explain who will have access to the data during the research and how access to data is controlled, especially in collaborative Partnerships.

Explain which institutional data protection policies are in place.

section.title: Protection and security of sensitive data

page.title: PERSONAL DATA, INTELECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND OWNERSHIP

fieldSet.title: Will you process and/or store personal data during your project?
How will compliance with legislation and (institutional) regulation on personal data be ensured?
fieldSet.description:
FCT strongly recommends that you seek advice from specialised support staff (DPO or equivalent) at your institution.

Ensure that when dealing with personal data data protection laws (for example GDPR) are complied with:
   -  Gain informed consent for preservation and/or sharing of personal data.
   -  Consider anonymisation of personal data for preservation and/or sharing (truly   anonymous data are no longer considered personal data).
   - Consider pseudonymisation of personal data (the main difference with anonymisation is that pseudonymisation is reversible).
    - Consider encryption which is seen as a special case of pseudonymisation (the encryption key must be stored separately from the data, for instance by a trusted third party).
    - Explain whether there is a managed access procedure in place for authorised users of personal data.

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: How will ownership of the data and intellectual property rights to the data be managed?
fieldSet.description:
Explain who will be the owner of the data, meaning who will have the rights to control access. 

Make sure to cover these matters of rights to control access to data for multi-partner projects and multiple data owners in the consortium agreement.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Responsible(s)”

section.title: How will you handle issues regarding the processing of personal information and intellectual property rights and ownership?

options.label: All data resulting from the project will be preserved for at least 10 years
options.value: All data resulting from the project will be preserved for at least 10 years

fieldSet.title: How will data be selected for long-term preservation?
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Indicate what data must be retained or destroyed for contractual, legal, or regulatory purposes.
Indicate how it will be decided what data to keep. 
Describe the data to be preserved long-term.

section.title: Long-term data preservation

options.label: All data resulting from the project will be made available
options.value: All data resulting from the project will be made available

fieldSet.title: What data will be made available for re-use?
Please specify.
fieldSet.description:
Indicate what data will be made available for re-use. 
This selection may differ from the data that is preserved, when the data are so large that it is unfeasible to deposit the data in a repository in its entirety, or if there are reasons that prohibit making data available for re-use. 
If there are any restrictions on the re-use of the data, or if it is necessary to restrict access to certain parts of the data or to apply a data sharing agreement, explain how and why. 
Explain what actions will be taken to overcome or to minimize restrictions
As much as possible, research data should be made publicly available for re-use. 

As a minimum, FCT requires that the data underpinning research papers should be made available to other researchers at the time of the article’s publication, unless there are valid reasons not to do so. The guiding principle here is 'as open as possible, as closed as necessary.' Due consideration is given to aspects such as privacy, public security, ethical limitations, property rights and commercial interests.

options.label: Data available as soon as archived in repository
options.value: Data available as soon as archived in repository

options.label: Data available as soon as article is published
options.value: Data available as soon as article is published

options.label: Data available upon completion of the project
options.value: Data available upon completion of the project

options.label: Data available after completion of project (with embargo)
options.value: Data available after completion of project (with embargo)

fieldSet.title: When will the data be available for re-use, and for how long will the data be available?
fieldSet.description:
Explain when the data will be made available. Indicate the expected timely release. 
Explain the reason and duration of any embargo periods. 
Explain whether exclusive use of the data will be claimed and if so, why and for how long. Indicate whether data sharing will be postponed or restricted for example to publish, protect intellectual property, or seek patents. 

As a minimum, FCT requires that the data underpinning research papers should be made available to other researchers at the time of the article’s publication, unless there are valid reasons not to do so.

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: In which repository will the data be archived and made available for re-use, and under which license?
fieldSet.description:
Indicate where the data will be deposited and made available for re-use. 
Repository Finder can help you find an appropriate repository to deposit your research data. 

Typically, appropriate data repositories adhere to the following minimum selection criteria:
  • provision of persistent and unique identifiers; 
  • use of metadata standards that are broadly accepted by the scientific community; 
  • provision of information that is publicly available; 
  • enabling access to data under well-specified conditions and following open and standard access protocols;
  • provision of information about licenses and permissions; ensuring persistence of data and metadata.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Repositories”

options.label: The repository is registered in the re3data directory
options.value: The repository is registered in the re3data directory

options.label: The repository is aggregated in OpenAIRE
options.value: The repository is aggregated in OpenAIRE

fieldSet.title: Please confirm if:

selectData.label: Does the repository support persistent identifiers?
Please identify the persistent identifier system from the list

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Please indicate whether a persistent identifier will be pursued.
fieldSet.description: Indicate whether a persistent identifier will be pursued. Typically, a trustworthy, long-term repository will provide a persistent identifier.

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually
Please state the date in which the license goes into effect

fieldSet.title: Under which license will the data be archived and made available for re-use?
fieldSet.description: Indicate under which license the data may be re-used. Check the commonly used Creative Commons licenses.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Licences”

fieldSet.title: Describe your strategy for publishing the analysis software that will be generated in this project.
fieldSet.description:
Indicate whether potential users need specific tools or software (technical resources, such as specific scripts, codes or algorithms developed during the project) to access, interpret and (re-)use the data.

Indicate how these items will be made available. 

Consider the sustainability of software needed for accessing and interpreting the data. Check the Five Recommendations for FAIR Software.

section.title: Data available for re-use

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually
Please specify the roles and responsibilities

fieldSet.title: Who (for example role, position, and institution) will be responsible for data management?
fieldSet.description:
Outline the roles and responsibilities for data management/stewardship activities, for example:
  • data capture;
  • metadata production;
  • data quality;
  • storage and backup;
  • data archiving;
  • and data sharing. 
For collaborative projects, explain the coordination of data management responsibilities across partners. 

Indicate who is responsible for implementing the DMP, and for ensuring it is reviewed and, if necessary, revised. 

Consider regular updates of the DMP.

multiplicity.placeholder: Please select this option in case you want to add more “Responsible(s)”

fieldSet.title: What resources (for example financial and time) will be dedicated to data management and ensuring that data will be FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable)?
fieldSet.description:
Explain how the necessary resources (for example time) to prepare the data for sharing/preservation (data curation) have been costed in. 

Consider and justify any resources needed to deliver the data. These may include storage costs, hardware, staff time, costs of preparing data for deposit, and repository charges. 

Indicate whether additional resources will be needed to prepare data for deposit or to cover any charges from data repositories. If yes, explain how much is needed and how such costs will be covered. 

Please elaborate on the budget in your FCT grant application, if appropriate.

section.title: Responsibilities and resources for data management

reference.label: Universidade de Coimbra Instituto de Ciências Nucleares Aplicadas à Saúde

field.textValue: Data will be stored at CIBIT, a research unit of the University of Coimbra. Data managing duties are responsibility of the PI, Bruno Direito. Data is owned by the University of Coimbra.

textListValues.textListValue: All data resulting from the project will be preserved for at least 10 years

field.textValue: Following the BIDS standard, we will add .json and .tsv sidecar files that include information regarding several details of the dataset, including participants, dataset, and task (according to the standard these files are named dataset-description.json, participants.json, participants.tsv).

The institution has a centralized VNA infrastructure (commercial solution by Siemens) that implements redundancy. There is no policy on data backup (either on-site and/or off-site). The raw data is kept in its original form in the VNA, and data is copied for statistical analysis.
Bruno Direito is responsible for data acquisition and data quality assurance and Ana Rodrigues is responsible for checking compliance with regulatory aspects.
CC0

textListValues.textListValue: Data available as soon as article is published

field.textValue: We will publish the data without resctrictions as soon as the first publication is published in a open peer-reviewed journal.
mixed media (image and textual)
Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS)
Considering the volume of data and current practices of the institution, most data managing tasks have been included in internal procedures and guidelines.

This project is based on brain imaging: the dataset will be saved in the BIDS format (The specification is intentionally based on simple file formats and folder structures to reflect current lab practices and make it accessible to a wide range of scientists coming from different backgrounds - BIDS is developed by the community for the community and everybody can become a part of the community, for more information, please consult https://bids.neuroimaging.io/)
structural and functional human brain imaging (BIDS standard) - .nii, .json and .tsv files

textListValues.textListValue: All data resulting from the project will be made available

field.textValue: We aim to acquire data from approximately 50 participants - each participant requires 2 GB
All processing scripts will be published in our intitutional GitHub account - https://github.com/CIBIT-UC.
OpenNeuro
Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS)

All participants signed an informed consent before data acquisition (previously approved by an ethics committee). Raw data is stored in a certified, commercial solution (Vendor Neutral Archive) with access policies in-place - Only approved personnel has access to project's data. All secondary copies (data extracted) is pseudo-anonymized. Only the Principal Investigator (PI) has access to the conversion table (id - project code). The table will be destroyed after the project end.

textListValues.textListValue: The repository is registered in the re3data directory
The repository is aggregated in OpenAIRE

field.textValue: .tsv files with structured information regarding the task, and acquisition parameters for each session (data regarding details of the task performed by each participant such as events onset and duration - this information is key for statistical data analysis). .json files with a structured information on the acquisition parameters (filename example for participant 01, session 01, run 01 - "sub-01_ses-01_run-1_T1w.json").
eng
